_comment: Deterministic fixture cases for scripts/keelplane_research_codex.py --self-test. Each case supplies canned per-phase outputs so the full Scope->Research->Synthesize->Verify->Compose chain runs WITHOUT live codex, exercising phase handoffs + the verdict-integrity invariant. The verify verdicts are chosen to hit every integrity branch: a real confirmed (evidence kept), an evidence-less confirmed (downgraded), a refuted, a hallucinated claim_id (dropped), and a claim with no verdict at all (uncovered).

cases.name: cache-eviction-smoke
cases.question: How does the widget cache evict entries?

angles.prompt: How are entries removed when the cache is full?
How is the cache capacity determined?

claims.statement: The cache evicts once it holds 100 entries.
The maximum size is configurable at construction.
The cache pre-allocates its backing store.

sections.title: Eviction
sections.body: The cache evicts via LRU (ev-1) once full (ev-2).

sections.title: Sizing
sections.body: Capacity is set at construction (sz-1) with pre-allocation (sz-2).

claims.statement: The cache evicts once it holds 100 entries.
The maximum size is configurable at construction.
The cache pre-allocates its backing store.

verdicts.reason: Looks plausible from the field name.
Capacity is a module constant, not a constructor argument.
Verdict for a claim id that was never in the draft.

fixtures.compose:
# Widget cache eviction

The cache uses LRU eviction: `self._store.move_to_end(key)` (src/cache.py:22).

## Unverified / open

- Refuted: the maximum size is NOT configurable at construction; it is a hardcoded constant (src/cache.py:10).
- Unverified: the 100-entry eviction threshold was not checked against a cited source.
- Uncovered: pre-allocation of the backing store received no verdict.

## Verification summary

confirmed 1 / partial 0 / refuted 1 / unverified 1 / uncovered 1
